figures.caption: Table 11: Summary of multivariate UEA benchmark datasets used in our experiments.
Table 10: Summary of univariate UCR benchmark datasets used in our experiments.
Table 21: Performance across 12 UCR datasets for LSTM → LSTM. Best per row in bold; second best is underlined. (For Avg. Rank, lower is better.)
Table 20: Detailed UCR results at earliness e ∈ {0.2L, 0.4L, 0.5L, 0.6L, 0.8L,L} for LSTM → LSTM. Best per row in bold.
Figure 2: Fidelity comparisons across methods and earliness levels, with each boxplot summarizing 12 UCR datasets. Fidelity is measured as teacher–student top-1 agreement on the test set.
Table 23: Detailed results for Inception → Inception on multivariate datasets under Time-wise partialness (e = 0.5L) and Time+Channel partialness (e = 0.5L,m = 0.5M ). Best result per row in bold.
Table 22: Detailed results for LSTM → LSTM on multivariate datasets under Time-wise partialness (e = 0.5L) and Time+Channel partialness (e = 0.5L,m = 0.5M ). Best result per row in bold.
Table 25: Detailed self-distillation results on 12 UCR datasets under three architectures. Best per row is in bold.
Table 24: Complete model compression results under two earliness levels (e = 0.5L,L) and two compression targets (LSTM3-100→LSTM1-8, LSTM3-100→LSTM2-32). Best per row is in bold.
Table 14: Training, memory, and inference cost comparison across distillation methods for LSTM3100 → LSTM3-100.

Figure 1: Depiction of (a) the proposed GDPD framework, and (b) a comparison of KD vs. GDPD. KD provides a single static teacher signal, whereas GDPD provides each student feature with a collection of diverse and progressive teacher signals over the course of training.
